status: first research and philosophy corpus consolidation pass
purpose: Classify the senses-and-sensibility corpus as research, philosophy, dissertation, paper, and Telos-theory substrate without treating it as shipped tooling or publication-ready scholarship.
claim_boundary: This registry is based on current local file reads plus live flagship receipts. It does not verify journal requirements, citations, page locators, institutional AI policy, authorship attestation, or philosophical correctness.

evidence.summary: Live flagship status tools returned MATCH before this subregistry was created.
Forum did not force a single lane; it treated the task as cross-lane research, technical writing, and Project Telos coordination.
Gather sealed the directory digest but dropped the large corpus payload from the model-facing catalog.
Core docs frame the corpus as live scholarship, pre-proof, AI-assisted research substrate, apparatus gated on human re-derivation, and citation-locator verification.
Current index prose references these artifacts, but the files were not present in the checkout during this pass.

rows.role: public research-corpus front door and navigation layer
rows.publication_target: site page or research corpus landing
rows.telos_overlap: Provides the philosophical vocabulary for accountability, authorship, human gate, evidence, and conferral.
rows.publication_boundary: Can be linked as a research corpus, but not cited as final dissertation, proof, or tool capability.
rows.current_action: Keep indexed; repair references to missing curation artifacts or restore those artifacts before stronger publication claims.

rows.maturity_label: AI-assisted integrated edition with explicit confidence, source, and human-ownership caveats
rows.telos_overlap: Explains the intrinsic accountability thesis that Telos implements as receipts, gates, and source-bound action.
rows.publication_boundary: Not submittable scholarship until a human re-derives, owns, and defends the claims under the stated method and institutional policy.
rows.current_action: Use only through summarized, non-overclaiming theory references in Telos docs.

rows.role: dissertation, viva, contribution, verdict, and adversarial audit apparatus
rows.maturity_label: research apparatus with explicit viva and authorship gates
rows.publication_target: dissertation plan and internal publication-prep corpus
rows.telos_overlap: Useful for Telos publication gates, proof packet non-claims, and human-ownership requirements.
rows.publication_boundary: Must not be treated as a completed institutional submission or authorship attestation.
rows.current_action: Convert its gates into Telos publication-control checklist rows.

rows.role: submission-shaped paper drafts and venue kits
rows.publication_target: journal paper candidates after live guideline, citation, anonymization, AI-disclosure, and authorship gates
rows.telos_overlap: Source material for arXiv/ORCiD-style methods and theory papers once gates clear.
rows.publication_boundary: Venue requirements, APCs, and policies are time-sensitive; this pass did not re-check live journal pages.
rows.current_action: Queue as paper candidates with blocker labels, not as publish-ready papers.

rows.maturity_label: working bibliography with confidence labels and locator caveats
rows.telos_overlap: Model for source discipline: no page numbers or exact locators unless independently verified.
rows.publication_boundary: Bibliography entries are not live citation verification or permission to assert page-specific claims.
rows.current_action: Adopt the citation-locator rule in Telos paper queues and source ledgers.

rows.role: preserved source substrate and discussion lineage
rows.publication_target: internal-source or cited corpus appendix after sanitization review
rows.telos_overlap: Useful for theory provenance, but too large and raw for Telos front-door docs.
rows.publication_boundary: Do not copy long source bodies into Telos docs; reference by path, digest, and summarized boundary only.
rows.current_action: Keep separate from tool docs and public product claims.

rows.publication_target: Telos theory note or methods appendix
rows.telos_overlap: Directly informs action receipts, authentication/authorization separation, human gate, revocation, and proof-before-trust language.
rows.publication_boundary: Do not infer operational security sufficiency or implementation maturity from the philosophy corpus.
rows.current_action: Use as conceptual rationale behind existing Telos receipts and proof packets, not as a substitute for tests.

rows.role: hostile review, cross-examination, and self-limiting verdict material
rows.publication_target: internal review packets and possible methods appendix
rows.telos_overlap: Useful pattern for Crucible-style verdicts, negative controls, and non-claim gates.
rows.publication_boundary: Agent/adversarial agreement is not independent proof; corpus itself warns against treating one corpus interrogating itself as independent witness.
rows.current_action: Translate the method into proof-packet critique patterns, not into philosophical truth claims.

rows.publication_target: quarantine-and-adapt until restored or references are corrected
rows.telos_overlap: Important because Telos docs should not point to absent curation apparatus as evidence.
rows.publication_boundary: Do not cite these artifacts as present evidence in current Telos docs until the files exist or the references are updated.
rows.current_action: Restore, regenerate, or patch references before public publication cleanup.

publication_gates: Human re-derivation gate: the author must be able to reconstruct and defend the claims without relying on the substrate.
Provenance ledger gate: contribution origin, candidate repair, citation verification, and AI assistance must be recorded before submission.
Live policy gate: journal requirements, APCs, AI-use policies, word limits, and blinding rules must be checked at submission time.
Citation locator gate: no page, volume, verse, or exact locator should be asserted unless independently verified against the edition used.
AI-use disclosure gate: material generative drafting must be disclosed in the venue-appropriate location.
Anonymization gate: submission kits must strip author-identifying corpus references for double-blind review.
Telos separation gate: philosophy/theory corpus can motivate tools, but tool capability requires separate receipts, tests, and verifier reports.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Publication gate import
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Add Senses human-gate, provenance-ledger, citation-locator, and AI-disclosure gates to the Telos publication queue rubric.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Theory-to-receipt vocabulary map
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Map authentication/authorization, human gate, non-aseity, witness, and conferral terms to Telos action-receipt vocabulary without overclaiming philosophy.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Adversarial review pattern
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Translate the attack/defense/verdict pattern into a reusable Crucible cleanroom-review packet for paper candidates.

telos_integration_priorities.name: Missing-artifact repair pass
telos_integration_priorities.first_artifact: Resolve or restore the absent curation artifacts named by current Senses index prose.

next_actions: Create a Telos publication gate checklist that imports the Senses human-ownership and citation-locator rules.
Open a targeted Senses repair pass for referenced-missing curation artifacts.
Decide whether Senses front-door docs should be updated in its own repo after rebasing its one-behind main branch.
Keep Senses theory references in Telos docs as hypotheses, rationale, or source substrate unless a separate Telos receipt proves an implementation claim.
Add a Telos subregistry next; it is the remaining dense mixed surface after Build, proof/witnessing, and Senses.
